**Task Documentation for Music Enthusiasts**  
This document will cover all the ways music enthusiasts can interact with this website. It will cover how to use the different tools and visualizations of the author’s favorite albums and artists. After reading through this documentation, you will be able to filter through, select, see rankings for, and compare different albums from different artists.  

**To begin, navigate to:** [https://cholstro.shinyapps.io/shiny-music/](https://cholstro.shinyapps.io/shiny-music/) 

*Filtering for number one albums throughout different years*

1. Click the **Number One Albums** tab at the top of the page.  
2. Locate the **Choose the Years** slider. This is how you can choose the years you are interested in.  
3. Drag either circle until the blue labels on top of the circles reflect the range of years you want to evaluate for top albums.

\*Note: If the circles are stacked on top of each other, it will only show top albums from that particular year.   
For example, if interested in the years 2022-2024, the interface appears like this: 

![][image1]

4. Repeat for any combination of years\!

*Select specific years to see album rankings*

1. Click the blue **Top Albums by Year** tab at the top of the page.  
2. Locate the **Choose a year** dropdown menu.  
3. On the right of the box labeled **1993**, click the triangle pointing down.  
4. Scroll through the year options and select the year you are interested in.  
5. Click the **submit** button under the menu. A ranked list of albums from the selected year is displayed with the top album appearing on top.

For year 2004, this is what the interface should look like: 

6. Repeat for any year\!

\*Note: You do not have to hit the submit button after the first time when selecting a new year. 

*How to choose a specific artist to view their album rankings*

1. Click the blue **Artists** **Albums** tab at the top of the page.  
2. Locate the **Choose a band or artist** dropdown menu.  
3. On the right of the box labeled **10,000 Maniacs**, click the triangle pointing down.  
4. Scroll through the alphabetized list of artists and select the artist you are interested in.   
5. Click the **submit** button under the menu. The output should display a list of that artist's albums, their year created, and their rating (1-10) as well as a summary of the number of albums ranked and that artist's average rating.

For example, if the **Arctic Monkeys** are selected, the output should look like this:

![][image2]

6. Repeat for any artist\!

\*Note: You do not have to hit the submit button after the first time when selecting a new artist. 

*Discover artists with a specific number of rankings* 

1. Click the blue **Favorite Artists** tab at the top of the page.  
2. Locate the **Minimum number of albums** dropdown menu.  
3. On the right of the box labeled **10**, click the triangle pointing down.  
4. Scroll through the numbers listed 1-10 and choose a number.

\*Note: These numbers represent artists with at least that number of albums ranked by the author. For example, if 2 is selected, only artists with at least 2 albums ranked will appear.

5. If only interested in studio albums, keep the **Exclude EPs and Live Albums** box checked. 
![][image3]  
   1. If interested in EPs and Live albums, click the check box once and these will be included in the output.   
6. Click the **submit button** under the menu.

For example, with a minimum of 10 albums and excluding EPs and live albums, the output should look like this: 
 ![][image4]

7. Repeat for any minimum number of albums\!

\*Note: You do not have to hit the submit button after the first time when selecting a new minimum number of albums. Similarly, the **Exclude EPs and Live Albums** box stays as it was for the previous submission.

*How to compare two artists and their album rankings*

1. Click the blue **Artist Comparison** tab at the top of the page.  
2. Locate the **First band or artist** dropdown menu.  
3. On the right of the box labeled **10,000 Maniacs**, click the triangle pointing down.  
4. Scroll through the list of artists and choose an artist.  
5. Locate the **Second band or artist** dropdown menu.  
   1. Repeat steps 3 and 4 with a new artist.

The graph at the bottom of the page should update with the first artist selected in red and the second in blue. The x axis represents the year each of the artists’ albums were created and the y axis represents the rating each of the albums received by the author.   
\*Note: if the same artist is selected twice, the graph will show the data in blue.  
If **Admiral Fallow** and **Ben Folds** are selected, the output should look like this:

![][image5]

6. Repeat for any two artists\!

*Filter for albums that the software creator does not own on vinyl*

1. Click the blue **Vinyl** tab at the top of the page.  
2. Locate the **Select which albums to display** dropdown menu.  
3. On the right of the box labeled **Only 10 ratings**, click the triangle pointing down.  
4. Scroll through the list of options listed.

\*Note: These options show a list of only albums with that number rating and above that the author does not own on vinyl. For example, if **8 and higher** is selected, only albums with 8, 9, or 10 ratings will be output. 

5. Click the **submit** button under the menu.

For example, if **Only 10 ratings** is selected, the output should look like this: 

![][image6]

6. Repeat for any minimum rating.

\*Note: You do not have to hit the submit button after the first time when selecting a new minimum rating.
